title: Eddy — fluid studio
description: A real-time, from-scratch incompressible Navier–Stokes fluid studio in pure TypeScript. Stable Fluids with semi-Lagrangian + MacCormack advection and FOUR pressure-projection Poisson solvers over one identical Neumann/obstacle stencil — red-black SOR, matrix-free Jacobi-preconditioned Conjugate Gradients, a from-scratch geometric MULTIGRID V-cycle (work-optimal O(N), grid-independent convergence), and MULTIGRID-PRECONDITIONED CG (MGCG) — plus OPEN inflow/outflow boundaries (zero-gradient velocity + Dirichlet pressure) that turn the tank into a true channel so a von Kármán wake leaves the domain instead of recirculating. Carries vorticity confinement, a Boussinesq temperature field driving real thermal convection, a reactive combustion model with variable-density buoyancy, RGB ink with its own Schmidt-number diffusivity, and arbitrary solid obstacles. A flagship LAGRANGIAN-COHERENT-STRUCTURE (FTLE) render mode integrates the flow map to expose the hidden transport skeleton — the attracting/repelling manifolds where dye collects or is flung apart. Sixteen scenes — the von Kármán vortex street (closed AND open-channel), Rayleigh–Bénard convection, a buoyant plume, Kelvin–Helmholtz shear, the lid-driven cavity, a self-sustaining Fire, the Taylor–Green vortex, decaying AND continuously-forced 2-D turbulence, and the double-shear-layer benchmark — plus a live SPECTRA LAB running a from-scratch 2-D FFT to plot the kinetic-energy spectrum E(k), its k^-3/k^-5/3 cascade slopes, AND the spectral energy flux Π(k) (negative ⇒ the 2-D inverse cascade) whose nonlinear transfer is provably energy-conserving (∑T(k)=0). Dye/speed/vorticity/pressure/temperature/LCS views, animated Line Integral Convolution, schlieren and Q-criterion vortex-core visualisation, streamline + tracer overlays, a live hover probe, WebM capture, and a 43-check numerical verification suite (incl. grid-independent multigrid, MGCG, analytic diffusion-decay and FTLE strain rates, FFT/Parseval identities, exact energy-transfer conservation, and the open-channel through-flow) that proves the solver's claims live in the browser.
